steps.title: Draw the central axis
steps.description: Lightly mark a vertical center line from top to bottom. Everything on this teapot is symmetrical left-to-right, so this axis is your anchor for every shape that follows.

steps.title: Block the body
steps.description: Sketch a rounded rectangle or barrel shape centered on the axis — wider than it is tall, with slightly flattened top and bottom. Think of it as a squashed sphere; avoid making it too perfect a circle.

steps.title: Add the lid ellipse and knob
steps.description: Across the top of the body, draw a shallow ellipse to represent the lid opening — this curve tells the viewer the teapot is three-dimensional. Sit a small dome and a tiny knob on top of it.

steps.title: Place the spout
steps.description: On the left side of the body, draw a gently curving tube that flares slightly at the tip. The spout should attach cleanly where it meets the body — no gap, no overlap; let it grow out of the silhouette.

steps.title: Place the handle
steps.description: On the right side, draw a C-shaped handle that echoes the curve of the body. Make sure both ends connect firmly at the same visual weight — a handle that tapers too much looks fragile.

steps.title: Add the base ring and refine contours
steps.description: Draw a thin ellipse at the bottom of the body for the footing ring. Then walk back through your lines: smooth the body silhouette, clean up where spout and handle attach, and erase the center axis.

steps.title: Suggest light and surface
steps.description: Add a highlight ellipse on the upper-left of the body and a cast shadow ellipse beneath the base. A few light parallel strokes on the right side of the body suggest the ceramic's curved surface catching light.
